title: pinus flexilis subsp. alpina
intro: Pinus flexilis subsp. alpina, also known as limber pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of North America, characterized by its flexible branches and dark green needles. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pinus flexilis subsp. alpina include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
